name: agentic_software_delivery_process
objective: Build and maintain software with human-readable intent, durable architecture decisions, scoped agent execution, machine-checkable verification, and easy debugging.

principles.name: Adaptive context comes first
principles.rule: Before planning or coding, the agent must assess the human's collaboration level, classify the work and risk, infer what it can from available sources, and produce a Ready-To-Proceed Packet.

principles.name: Intent is a first-class artifact
principles.rule: Every initiative must have a durable PRD before planning implementation.

principles.name: Plans are products
principles.rule: Plans must be reviewable, executable, versioned, and reconciled.

principles.name: Small PRs are the unit of delivery
principles.rule: Each PR should represent one scoped task that can be reviewed comfortably.

principles.name: Verification must be machine-checkable
principles.rule: Each task must define commands, expected results, and required artifacts.

principles.name: Debuggability is designed before implementation
principles.rule: Each feature must define logs, metrics, traces, errors, and runbooks when relevant.

principles.name: Agents need boundaries
principles.rule: Each agent task must define scope, non-scope, stop conditions, and review criteria.

principles.name: Security claims require evidence
principles.rule: Every security-sensitive claim must be backed by tests, threat model coverage, access review, scan output, configuration proof, or adversarial review notes.

principles.name: Technology choices are governed
principles.rule: Agents may recommend technology choices, but humans approve languages, frameworks, cloud services, vendors, operational tools, observability, logging, and cost commitments.

principles.name: Strategy precedes large PRDs
principles.rule: Large initiatives must link PRDs to vision, strategy, methods, measures, non-negotiables, anti-goals, and decision boundaries.

adr_policy.alternative_for_small_decisions: Record in Execution Brief or PR Decision Notes.

roles.name: Execution Agent
roles.model_guidance: Use faster or cheaper model only when the PR task packet is precise.

roles.name: Review Agent
roles.constraint: Should be distinct from the execution agent for high-risk work.

intake_and_handoff_policy.purpose: Gather human, business, technical, budget, and operational context before Phase 1, adapting the interview to the human's technical comfort and the work's risk.

work_type_classification_policy.purpose: Classify the work during intake so the process applies the right evidence and gates.

ai_execution_strategy_policy.purpose: Budget reasoning, coding, testing, review, integration, and security separately.

exception_policy.purpose: Allow explicit, owned shortcuts without silent risk acceptance.

lifecycle_handoff_rule: Use Ready-To-Proceed Packets, PR Task Packets, and handoff packets to preserve context at the right weight. Create handoff packets at major phase boundaries, risk escalations, or material scope, budget, timeline, or safety changes. Do not proceed when blocking questions remain or when material budget, timeline, or risk changes lack human approval.

technology_strategy_policy.purpose: Prevent agents from making uncomfortable or costly technology decisions without human approval.

vision_strategy_methods_policy.purpose: Connect large initiatives to company, org, or product vision before PRD creation.

release_target_policy.purpose: Define the smallest coherent release and prevent overbuilding.

definitions.release_target: The smallest coherent version that produces useful value and can be reviewed, tested, released, and rolled back.
definitions.roadmap: The ordered set of later increments, each tied to a reason, dependency, or learning milestone.
definitions.deferral_contract: A written agreement that a capability is intentionally excluded from the current release, including the trigger for revisiting it.

forked_exploration_policy.purpose: Detect dead ends early by testing multiple plausible approaches in bounded parallel spikes before production implementation.

lifecycle.name: Agent Context Intake
lifecycle.purpose: Turn the human's initial request into enough shared context to choose the right process path.

lifecycle.name: Vision, Strategy, and Methods
lifecycle.purpose: Define strategic direction, operating methods, success measures, and decision boundaries for large initiatives before product planning begins.

lifecycle.name: Direction
lifecycle.purpose: Decide whether the work should exist.

lifecycle.name: Release Target and Roadmap
lifecycle.purpose: Define the next shippable increment and what will be deferred.

lifecycle.name: Technology Strategy and Platform Constraints
lifecycle.purpose: Define the approved technology posture before agents make costly or uncomfortable implementation choices.

lifecycle.name: Execution Brief
lifecycle.purpose: Translate product intent and release scope into first-pass implementation strategy.

lifecycle.name: Architecture Map
lifecycle.purpose: Explain system boundaries, components, flows, interfaces, and operational model.

lifecycle.name: Forked Exploration
lifecycle.purpose: Run bounded parallel spikes for high-uncertainty decisions before production implementation.

lifecycle.name: Task Graph
lifecycle.purpose: Break work into dependency-aware execution units.

lifecycle.name: PR Task Packet
lifecycle.purpose: Make the PR the execution contract.

lifecycle.name: Implementation
lifecycle.purpose: Build the smallest reviewable change that satisfies the PR task packet.

lifecycle.name: Verification
lifecycle.purpose: Prove the change works and is safe to review.

lifecycle.name: Review
lifecycle.purpose: Evaluate the change against intent, architecture, scope, and risk.

lifecycle.name: Operate
lifecycle.purpose: Learn whether the shipped change works in reality.

lifecycle.name: Reconcile
lifecycle.purpose: Keep plans, code, docs, and reality aligned.

artifact_format_policy.durable_knowledge: Markdown with YAML frontmatter for PRDs, briefs, architecture maps, ADRs, runbooks, memory, process exceptions, indexes, and logs.
artifact_format_policy.machine_contracts: JSON for Ready-To-Proceed Packets, Mini PR Task Packets, PR Task Packets, and Task Graphs.
artifact_format_policy.strictness: Validate JSON contracts strictly. Consume Markdown knowledge permissively while preserving unknown frontmatter keys.
